title: The Impeachment Process in the Senate
summary: After the House impeaches a federal officer, the Senate may conduct a trial to determine if the individual should be removed from office. The Senate has a set of rules specific to the conduct of an impeachment trial, most of which originated in the early 19th century. The impeachment rules lay out specific steps that the Senate takes to organize for a trial. House managers (Members of the House who present the case...

title: Understanding How Impact Fees Shape Housing Development Feasibility and Local Fiscal Revenue
summary: High impact fees contribute meaningfully to housing production costs in California. A 25-percent reduction enables hundreds of new projects in high-cost cities; increased property and sales tax revenue offsets forgone fees in four to seven years.

title: Cultivating a Warrior Ethos in the Air Force
summary: RAND researchers examined how the U.S. Air Force can cultivate an Airminded Warrior identity, finding that the concept requires more development. They recommend several actions to strengthen this identity’s development in initial training and beyond.

title: Looking Beyond the Government’s Regulatory Toolkit
summary: Transformative artificial intelligence presents safety and societal risks and has prospective benefits. The authors develop a structured framework for how industry and civil society can help manage and mitigate those risks.

title: Another Hormuz? The Red Sea’s Threat to the Global Economy
summary: The Red Sea is vital to the free flow of global trade, but the Yemen-based Houthis and the fallout from the Iran war threaten to choke off the Mideast waterway, with major consequences for the global economy.

title: U.S.-Colombia Relations
summary: 1903 – 2026 Over the two centuries since Colombia’s independence, the relationship between Washington and Bogotá has evolved into a close economic and security partnership. But it has at times been strained by U.S. intervention, Cold War geopolitics, and the war on drugs.

Desai, visiting fellow at Stanford’s Hoover Institution. “The chemistry is real, and Trump genuinely likes Modi, but that isn’t addressing the real frictions or the eroding trust beneath them,” said Desai.

title: Beyond Brexit—and back to Europe
summary: Those who follow only intergovernmental negotiations or debates in the media might think that nothing has changed in the decade since the United Kingdom's voters decided to leave the European Union. Yet new polling finds that large majorities on both sides of the English Channel now support re-engagement.

title: Trump cancels signing ceremony for bipartisan housing bill, again upending GOP agenda
summary: Republicans hoped to celebrate passage of a bipartisan spending bill on Wednesday — until President Trump scuttled their plans. It was the latest in a series of rifts between Trump and his party.

title: Mayor Mamdani endorsed three House candidates. They all won
summary: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Zohran Mamdani, the mayor of New York City, the day after all three Congressional candidates he endorsed won their Democratic primary elections.

title: Trump upends bipartisan housing bill, leaving lawmakers scrambling
summary: President Trump has cancelled a signing planned for today of the 21st Century Road to Housing Act, the largest housing affordability in decades passed with overwhelming bipartisan support in Congress.

title: Postal Service says its cash crisis is delayed until at least 2031, but problems loom
summary: The U.S. Postal Service is no longer set to be out of cash in 2027, the agency's head says. But its finances remain shaky as Trump officials keep putting it in political hot water.

title: Will Texas' new top voting official be a 'disruptor'? Locals are preparing for it
summary: Just ahead of closely contested midterms, Texas is about to get a new top voting official. Many locals there fear the frontrunner is a state lawmaker and pastor with no election experience.

title: Progressive victories signal mood of some Democratic voters ahead of midterms
summary: Progressive Democrats dominated New York's midterm primary elections in a resounding show of strength for the Democratic Socialist mayor of New York City. All three candidates endorsed by Mayor Zohran Mamdani won their races, two of them ousting sitting congressmen. Geoff Bennett discussed the results with Brigid Bergin, a politics reporter for New York Public Radio.

title: How the Trump administration is trying to reshape how elections are run
summary: The Trump administration has made multiple efforts to reshape how elections are run with just months before the midterms. The debate over election security has led to tension between the White House and election administrators as the president falsely claims voter fraud is rampant nationwide. Liz Landers discussed more with Gabe Sterling of the Georgia Secretary of State's office.

title: White House seeks $87.6B from Congress for Iran war costs, U.S. farmers and Ebola response
summary: The White House has formally requested $87.6 billion mostly to replenish the Pentagon after the U.S. war against Iran, submitting the request to Congress at a politically difficult time as Republican and Democratic lawmakers have objected to any further military action.

title: WATCH: Congress dedicates national time capsule ahead of America's 250th anniversary
summary: The Time Capsule Act, a 2016 law creating the nonpartisan America250 commission mandated that a time capsule be buried in Philadelphia on July 4, 2026, and dug up 250 years later in 2276.

title: Quality and Patient Safety Review Processes in Military Treatment Facilities and Operational Clinical Services
summary: RAND researchers assessed the quality and patient safety review processes for care delivered in military treatment facilities and via operational clinical services. Findings were used to identify potential opportunities for improvement.

title: U.S. Veterans and Psychedelics
summary: In this report, the authors present new data on U.S veterans’ use of specific psychedelic substances (such as psilocybin mushrooms, LSD, and ibogaine), their psychedelics policy preferences, and how these compare with nonveterans.

title: SBA Disaster Loan Limits: Policy Options and Considerations
summary: The Small Business Administration (SBA) Disaster Loan Program provides disaster loans to eligible households and businesses to help them rebuild and recover after a disaster.
